description: Spain's capital is a world-class city without the tourist fatigue — the Prado holds Europe's finest art collection, the food scene rivals anywhere on earth, and madrileños genuinely don't go to dinner before 10pm.

neighborhoodRecs: Malasaña — Hipster central, independent cafes, vintage shops, young crowd, great for solo travellers.
Lavapiés — Madrid's most multicultural neighbourhood, incredible cheap food from around the world, gritty but authentic.
Chueca — LGBTQ+ neighbourhood, lively tapas bars, boutique shopping, very safe and vibrant.
Salamanca — Upscale, designer shopping, quieter restaurants, Madrid's equivalent of the Upper East Side.
areasToAvoid: Calle Montera and surrounding streets — known for street prostitution and occasional drug activity.
Gran Vía at 3–4am — drunk crowds and opportunistic thieves around the clubs.
Rastro flea market with a bulging wallet — crowds are dense and pickpockets work in teams.
scamWarnings: Three-card monte (trile) around El Rastro on Sundays — the crowd watching are all accomplices, you cannot win.
Overpriced flamenco shows near the tourist zones — book through your hotel or a reputable agency for authentic tablaos.
Taxi from Barajas airport: always use the official rank. The fixed rate to the city centre is €33 — any driver asking more is overcharging.
ATMs on Las Ramblas equivalent (Gran Vía) sometimes have card skimmers. Use ATMs inside banks.

airportTransfer.cheapest: Metro Line 8 from Terminal 4 to Nuevos Ministerios (city connection hub) — €4.50 airport supplement + €1.50 base = €6. Takes 25 min.
airportTransfer.fastest: Taxi — €33 fixed rate to any point in the city (M-30 zone), takes 20–30 min.
airportTransfer.recommended: Metro for budget travellers (easy, flat rate, frequent). Taxi for families or anyone with heavy luggage.
airportTransfer.taxiEstimate: €33 fixed rate city centre. €55–80 to destinations outside the M-30 ring road.

tippingEtiquette: Tipping is optional but appreciated. Leave €1–3 per person at restaurants, 10% for excellent service.
Cafes and bars: rounding up is the norm, no pressure.
Madrid eats late — lunch is 2–4pm, dinner 9:30–11pm. Showing up before 9pm for dinner is very touristy.
Greeting: handshake for strangers, two-cheek kiss (left cheek first) for anyone you've met before. Common even between men and women.
Sunday is sacred — many small shops are closed, but the Rastro flea market and Retiro Park are lively.

dailyCostBreakdown.accommodation: Hostel dorm €18–28 | Mid-range hotel €80–140 | Boutique hotel €160–300
dailyCostBreakdown.meals: Menú del día €10–14 | Tapas dinner €18–30 | Fine dining €60+
dailyCostBreakdown.transport: Metro single €1.50–2 | 10-trip card €11.35 | Taxi across city €7–12
dailyCostBreakdown.activities: Prado Museum €15 | Reina Sofía Museum €12 | Retiro Park free | Flamenco show €35–60

packingTips: Light clothing for summer — Madrid is inland and gets very hot (35°C+ in July/August).
A warm layer for winter — temperatures drop to 2–5°C at night in January.
Comfortable walking shoes — the city centre is very walkable on flat streets.
Pocket fan for summer — the heat is dry but relentless.
premium.bookingStrategy: May, June, and October are the sweet spots — good weather, fewer tourists than Barcelona. Avoid August when madrileños leave on holiday and many restaurants close. Prado tickets can be booked online to skip the queue.
localTips: The Prado is free every day from 6–8pm — queue by 5:30pm for a reasonable wait.
Bocadillo de calamares (squid sandwich) at Bar La Campana near Plaza Mayor — €4, genuinely legendary.
Mercado de San Miguel is tourist central and overpriced. Head to Mercado de Antón Martín in Lavapiés instead.
Madrid's churros con chocolate at Chocolatería San Ginés (open 24 hours) after a night out is a rite of passage.
Retiro Park rowing boats on the lake — €8 for 45 minutes, magical on a sunny afternoon.
premium.bestTimeToVisit: May or October: mild 18–24°C, low crowds, all museums fully open, terrace culture in full swing.
dayTrips: Toledo — Medieval walled city 30 min by AVE train, El Greco paintings, stunning cathedral, day trip perfection.
Segovia — Roman aqueduct and Cinderella castle (inspiration for Disney), 30 min by AVE, great roast suckling pig.
El Escorial — Philip II's monastery-palace complex, an hour by Cercanías train, fascinating Habsburg history.
Ávila — the best-preserved medieval walls in Spain encircling a beautiful walled city, 1.5 hours by train.
Cuenca — a dramatic UNESCO city of 'hanging houses' perched above a gorge, 55 minutes by high-speed train.
visaInfo: EU/EEA citizens: no visa required — Spain is a full Schengen member with free movement for EU passport holders
UK citizens (post-Brexit): visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day Schengen period; passport must be valid for the duration of stay
US, Canadian, and Australian citizens: visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day Schengen period; no tourist visa required
Most other nationalities: consult the Spanish Ministry of Foreign Affairs; many nationalities require a Schengen visa obtained in advance from a Spanish consulate
The 90-day Schengen allowance is shared across all 27 Schengen member states — time in France, Italy, or any other Schengen country counts against the same quota
currencyTips: Currency: Euro (€) — Spain is a eurozone member; no currency exchange needed if arriving from another eurozone country
Cards are widely accepted in Madrid restaurants, hotels, and shops; contactless payment is the norm at most modern venues
Best ATMs: use bank-branded ATMs from CaixaBank, BBVA, or Santander — avoid standalone tourist ATMs near Puerta del Sol and Gran Vía which charge higher fees
Avoid: dynamic currency conversion (DCC) at card terminals — always choose to pay in euros
Cash is useful for tapas bars, the Rastro flea market, and small local cafés; keep €5–€20 notes on hand
premium.simCard: Movistar, Vodafone Spain, Orange, and MásMóvil are the main Spanish carriers. Tourist SIMs with 15–20 GB data cost €10–20 and include EU roaming — buy at Madrid Barajas Airport (MAD) Arrivals or at carrier stores around Gran Vía. For instant setup before landing, Airalo offers Spain eSIMs.
